name: research
description: Use for source-grounded research — REPORT (open-ended topic primer/lit review) or OPPORTUNITY (validate named product/business/market/feature with FADR memo). Triggers: 'research X', 'primer on X', 'validate whether building X is a good opportunity', 'should we build X

inspired_by.name: Systematic Approaches to a Successful Literature Review
inspired_by.author: Andrew Booth, Anthea Sutton, Diana Papaioannou
inspired_by.contribution: SALSA framework — Search, AppraisaL, Synthesis, Analysis — as the four required phases of any literature-grounded research report. Adapted here as the workflow spine: plan-search → triage → synthesize → confidence-and-limitations.

inspired_by.name: Guidelines for Performing Systematic Literature Reviews in Software Engineering
inspired_by.author: Barbara Kitchenham, Stuart Charters
inspired_by.contribution: Protocol-driven review: explicit research question, inclusion/exclusion criteria stated up front, search strategy documented, quality assessment per source. Adapted as the deep-dive depth mode and the 'one-sentence research question with explicit inclusions and exclusions' requirement.

inspired_by.name: Guidelines for Snowballing in Systematic Literature Studies
inspired_by.author: Claes Wohlin
inspired_by.contribution: Snowballing — forward and backward citation chasing from anchor sources — as a search strategy that complements database queries and is particularly well-suited to LLM-driven research where coverage matters more than reproducibility. Adapted as the deep-dive search behavior.

inspired_by.name: PRISMA 2020 statement
inspired_by.author: Matthew J. Page, Joanne E. McKenzie, Patrick M. Bossuyt
inspired_by.contribution: Reporting standard for systematic reviews: what every review must disclose (search strategy, exclusions, limitations) so readers can assess validity. Adapted as the Output requirements section and the report template's required fields.

inspired_by.name: A typology of reviews: an analysis of 14 review types and associated methodologies
inspired_by.author: Maria J. Grant, Andrew Booth
inspired_by.contribution: Fourteen review types differentiated by purpose, search, appraisal, synthesis, and analysis. Distilled here to three depth modes — brief (narrative orientation), survey (scoping/mapping), deep-dive (systematic). The depth router reflects this typology rather than treating 'research' as one undifferentiated request.

inspired_by.name: Opportunity Research Taxonomy (convo.txt)
inspired_by.author: User-curated notes, this repo
inspired_by.contribution: The 14-area taxonomy, the four-layer Facts/Assumptions/Decisions/Risks frame, the minimum-viable first-pass subset of six areas (Customer / Market / Competitive / Domain / Technical / Financial), and the 'a research branch that ends in notes but not decisions is just organized procrastination' decision-gate principle.

inspired_by.name: The Lean Startup
inspired_by.author: Eric Ries
inspired_by.contribution: Build-Measure-Learn loop; validated learning; pivot vs persevere as a forward-commitment decision rather than a vibe.

inspired_by.name: Know Your Customers' Jobs to Be Done
inspired_by.author: Clayton M. Christensen, Taddy Hall, Karen Dillon, David S. Duncan
inspired_by.contribution: Jobs-to-be-done framing: name the job, the context, and what is being fired. Competing-against-non-consumption frames substitutes and do-nothing as first-class competitors.

inspired_by.name: The Five Competitive Forces That Shape Strategy
inspired_by.author: Michael E. Porter
inspired_by.contribution: Industry attractiveness as a function of rivalry + buyer power + supplier power + substitutes + new-entrant threat. A market can be big and still unattractive.

inspired_by.name: Crossing the Chasm (3rd ed.)
inspired_by.author: Geoffrey A. Moore
inspired_by.contribution: Technology-adoption lifecycle; early-market vs mainstream gap; beachhead segment — pick one tightly-defined initial segment whose adoption proves the next segment is winnable.

inspired_by.name: Obviously Awesome
inspired_by.author: April Dunford
inspired_by.contribution: Positioning as deliberate context-setting: who is this for, what reference frame should the buyer hold the product against, and why those tradeoffs matter to them. Defaults are someone else's positioning.

inspired_by.name: Four Fits for $100M+ Growth
inspired_by.author: Brian Balfour
inspired_by.contribution: Beyond product/market fit, scale requires product/channel fit, channel/model fit, and model/market fit. A mismatch on any pair caps growth.

inspired_by.name: SaaS Metrics 2.0
inspired_by.author: David Skok
inspired_by.contribution: Canonical SaaS unit economics: CAC, LTV, payback period, gross margin, magic number, LTV/CAC > 3, payback < 12 months as common benchmarks.

inspired_by.name: Domain-Driven Design
inspired_by.author: Eric Evans
inspired_by.contribution: Ubiquitous language; bounded contexts; the cost of getting the domain model wrong. Domain understanding precedes design; the glossary is load-bearing, not decoration.

inspired_by.name: Antifragile
inspired_by.author: Nassim Nicholas Taleb
inspired_by.contribution: Fragile / robust / antifragile classification; concentration risk; convexity. Risk is structural, not just probabilistic.

inspired_by.name: Continuous Discovery Habits
inspired_by.author: Teresa Torres
inspired_by.contribution: Opportunity solution tree; weekly customer touchpoints; assumption tests vs discovery — research is a habit, not a phase.

inspired_by.name: Performing a Project Premortem
inspired_by.author: Gary Klein
inspired_by.contribution: Imagine the project failed in 12–18 months; work backwards to causes. Catches dissent that doesn't surface in current-state reviews. Used as a heuristic within `risk` here; the dedicated `premortem` skill runs the full interview.

inspired_by.name: Just Enough Research (2nd ed.)
inspired_by.author: Erika Hall
inspired_by.contribution: Right-sized research; 'research is just having the answers'; the explicit multiple-types-of-research taxonomy (organizational, user, design, competitive, evaluative).

inspired_by.name: Zero to One
inspired_by.author: Peter Thiel
inspired_by.contribution: Last-mover advantage; secrets (what important truth do very few people agree with you on?); the monopoly-vs-competition frame; build durable moats rather than chase parity.

inspired_by.name: Technological Revolutions and Financial Capital
inspired_by.author: Carlota Perez
inspired_by.contribution: Five surges of technological revolution; installation period vs deployment period; long-wave timing frames for trend analysis.

inspired_by.name: Diátaxis Documentation Framework
inspired_by.author: Daniele Procida
inspired_by.contribution: Four-quadrant separation (tutorial / how-to / reference / explanation). Applied to opportunity research: 14 area artifacts are reference + explanation; tutorials and how-tos live elsewhere. Don't mix kinds in one artifact.

inspired_by.name: The Only Thing That Matters
inspired_by.author: Marc Andreessen
inspired_by.contribution: Market quality dominates team and product; product/market fit threshold; the market 'pulls' a fit-finding product.

inspired_by.name: Stripe Atlas Guides
inspired_by.author: Stripe
inspired_by.contribution: Operational and legal patterns for early-stage businesses: entity formation, sales tax exposure, international expansion, contracts, IP, payment compliance.

inspired_by.name: How to Get Startup Ideas (and the Y Combinator essay series)
inspired_by.author: Paul Graham
inspired_by.contribution: Live-in-the-future framing; organic vs made-up ideas; founder-as-her-own-customer; the trap of plausible-but-unimportant problems.

inspired_by.name: Architecture Tradeoff Analysis Method (ATAM)
inspired_by.author: Rick Kazman, Mark Klein, Paul Clements (SEI)
inspired_by.contribution: Quality-attribute tradeoffs as named scenarios; sensitivity points; risk themes. Adapted here to frame build-vs-buy and architecture-option decisions inside the technical playbook.

inspired_by.name: Platform-Dependence and Growth Loops (Reforge)
inspired_by.author: Andrew Chen
inspired_by.contribution: Channel platform-dependence as a first-class risk class; growth loops (compounding mechanisms) as the scalability test for any channel.

metadata.catalog_summary: Source-grounded research in **two decision-frames**. `report` — open-ended research on a topic, with citations and no decision attached (primer, literature review, state-of-the-art; depth modes `brief` / `survey` / `deep-dive`). `opportunity` — validate a named product/business/market/feature opportunity across 14 areas (market, customer, competitive, domain, technical, data, operational, financial, legal, channel, GTM, stakeholder, risk, trend), ending in an F/A/D/R go/no-go/pivot decision with sub-agent fan-out per area. Every load-bearing claim is cited or marked as inference (report frame); every branch ends in a decision (opportunity frame). Provenance in [`skills/research/skill.json`](./skills/research/skill.json).
